trial.input_snapshot:
Create a structured memory entry:
- title: "Test Learning"
- memory_type: procedural
- scope: project
- project: "note-lark"
- status: structured
- confidence: 0.9
- tags: ["test", "learning"]
- body: "This is a test learning about note-lark."

Use memory_structured with payload wrapper.
Note: memory_structured ALWAYS creates a learning type note.

messages.content:
Create a structured memory entry:
- title: "Test Learning"
- memory_type: procedural
- scope: project
- project: "note-lark"
- status: structured
- confidence: 0.9
- tags: ["test", "learning"]
- body: "This is a test learning about note-lark."

Use memory_structured with payload wrapper.
Note: memory_structured ALWAYS creates a learning type note.

```json
{
  "payload": {
    "title": "Test Learning",
    "memory_type": "procedural",
    "scope": "project",
    "project": "note-lark",
    "status": "structured",
    "confidence": 0.9,
    "tags": [
      "test",
      "learning"
    ],
    "body": "This is a test learning about note-lark."
  }
}
```

details.reasoning: The agent correctly structured the memory entry with all required fields and values. However, the rubric explicitly requires 'memory_structured called with payload wrapper', which would mean the agent should indicate they are calling the memory_structured function together with the payload wrapper. The agent only provided the JSON payload wrapper without showing that memory_structured was being called.
